context: Current closing price of AutoZone (AZO): $3582.50 USD as of 2026-04-20.
justification_text:
My point estimates reflect a modest upward drift across horizons, anchored around current S&P 500 levels in the mid-3500s to low-3600s range. At the one-day horizon, the estimate of 3590 is largely a near-random-walk assumption with a slight mean-reversion pull, since markets tend to exhibit very little directional predictability over such short windows. The one-week estimate edges up marginally to 3605, reflecting a small positive equity risk premium that compounds over time but is nearly swamped by noise at that horizon. The one-month estimate of 3630 continues that gentle drift, consistent with a long-run expected return of roughly 7–8% annualized, which translates to only modest gains over a single month. None of these estimates embed a strong directional view — they are close to a flat projection because near-term macro signals were mixed at the time of forecasting.

The confidence interval widths reflect well-established empirical regularities in equity volatility. At one day, annualized S&P 500 volatility of roughly 18–20% implies a daily standard deviation of about 1.1–1.3%, so the ±80-point band around 3590 is consistent with roughly 1.5 standard deviations — a reasonable 90% interval. Over one week, volatility scales with the square root of time, widening the interval to ±175 points. At one month, the interval grows further to roughly ±380 points, capturing a wider range of macro and earnings outcomes. These widths also reflect elevated macro uncertainty at the time, including interest rate trajectory ambiguity and geopolitical risk, which justified somewhat wider intervals than pure historical volatility alone might suggest.

Specific risks that could push outcomes outside these intervals include a sudden Federal Reserve policy surprise — either a pivot toward cuts or an unexpectedly aggressive hike — which could move markets 3–5% in a single session, well beyond my one-day range. A significant credit event, banking sector stress, or a geopolitical escalation could similarly produce tail moves. On the upside, a strong resolution of trade tensions, a softer-than-expected inflation print, or a coordinated central bank easing signal could drive the index above 4010 within a month. Liquidity conditions also matter: during periods of thin market depth, moves can overshoot fundamentally justified levels significantly, making the tails fatter in practice than any symmetric interval implies.
